deep_dive.title: Gaze2Act: Gaze-Conditioned Vision-Language-Action Policies for Interactive Robot Manipulation
deep_dive.abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have recently shown strong potential for robot learning by following language instructions. However, in practice, language alone is often insufficient to precisely convey human intent. It is difficult to describe which exact object to interact with among similar candidates, where to act on the object, or how the target may change during execution. To address this limitation, we propose Gaze2Act, a novel VLA framework that leverages human gaze as a dynamic and intuitive intent signal for complex interactive manipulation. Gaze2Act first bridges the ego-exo view gap by mapping first-person gaze into the robot's perspective through cross-view semantic matching, producing both an object mask and a gaze point for coarse-to-fine target specification. These cues are then integrated into the policy through perception-level prompting and action-level conditioning, allowing the robot to attend to relevant regions and execute precise interactions under dynamic intent. In a systematic evaluation across seven task categories and 16 real-robot tasks on a Unitree G1 humanoid, Gaze2Act achieves state-of-the-art performance in both intent accuracy and task success rate. It notably outperforms baselines in object disambiguation, fine-grained interaction, and dynamic intent steering. These results demonstrate that human gaze provides a natural, low-burden, and highly expressive modality for human-in-the-loop VLA control.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: uses human gaze as dynamic intent signal for robot manipulation, directly addressing multimodal intent prediction
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用人类凝视作为动态意图信号进行机器人操作，直接解决多模态意图预测
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用人类凝视作为动态意图信号进行机器人操作，直接解决多模态意图预测
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用人类凝视作为动态意图信号进行机器人操作，直接解决多模态意图预测
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Gaze2Act proposes a gaze-conditioned VLA framework that uses human gaze as an intuitive intent signal for interactive robot manipulation, achieving precise intent grounding.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: Gaze2Act针对语言指令难以精确传达人类意图的问题，提出了一种凝视条件化的视觉-语言-动作（VLA）框架。该方法将第一人称凝视映射到机器人视角，生成物体掩码和注意力引导，使机器人能够理解用户关注的物体和操作位置。实验证明，凝视作为多模态意图信号显著提升了操作成功率和交互自然度，为实时眼动意图识别提供了有效范式。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: Gaze2Act针对语言指令难以精确传达人类意图的问题，提出了一种凝视条件化的视觉-语言-动作（VLA）框架。该方法将第一人称凝视映射到机器人视角，生成物体掩码和注意力引导，使机器人能够理解用户关注的物体和操作位置。实验证明，凝视作为多模态意图信号显著提升了操作成功率和交互自然度，为实时眼动意图识别提供了有效范式。

deep_dive.title: Intent Signal Theory: A Computational Framework for Intent-State Control in Human-AI Interaction
deep_dive.abstract: Current AI interaction models treat the prompt as the primary object of exchange, omitting a critical layer: the user's latent source intent, the goal state preceding and motivating the prompt. Here we introduce Intent Signal Theory (IST), a computational framework that formalises this missing intent layer. IST distinguishes four objects routinely conflated: latent source intent (I*), observable intent proxy (I-hat), encoded carrier (P), and model output (O). It formalises dimensional weights, encoding masks, structural and fidelity recovery scores, and public-private intent decomposition. The Theorem of Irreversible Intent Loss establishes that private intent absent from the carrier cannot be recovered beyond generic substitution. Evidence from four companion studies spanning six LLMs, three languages and three task domains shows structural-fidelity splits, human-validated metric dissociation, and weight-tolerance plateaus consistent with IST's predictions. IST reframes prompt engineering as intent-protocol design and identifies a computational layer that current AI systems lack.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: computational framework for intent-state control in human-AI interaction
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 人机交互中意图状态控制的计算框架
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 人机交互中意图状态控制的计算框架
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 人机交互中意图状态控制的计算框架
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Intent Signal Theory introduces a computational framework that formalizes the latent intent layer in human-AI interaction, distinguishing four objects and including theorems about intent loss. This directly supports the development of real-time intent recognition algorithms.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 当前AI交互模型忽略了用户潜在意图这一关键层次。本文提出意图信号理论（IST），一个形式化用户意图状态的计算框架，区分了潜在源意图、可观察意图代理、编码载体和模型输出四个对象，并定义了维度权重、编码掩码、结构及保真度恢复评分等概念。IST的不可逆意图损失定理揭示了私有意图无法被完全恢复的根本限制。该工作为设计面向真实意图感知的交互系统提供了理论基础，尤其对基于眼动等信号进行实时意图识别具有重要方法论价值。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 当前AI交互模型忽略了用户潜在意图这一关键层次。本文提出意图信号理论（IST），一个形式化用户意图状态的计算框架，区分了潜在源意图、可观察意图代理、编码载体和模型输出四个对象，并定义了维度权重、编码掩码、结构及保真度恢复评分等概念。IST的不可逆意图损失定理揭示了私有意图无法被完全恢复的根本限制。该工作为设计面向真实意图感知的交互系统提供了理论基础，尤其对基于眼动等信号进行实时意图识别具有重要方法论价值。

deep_dive.title: Attentional selection is a neuroeconomic decision
deep_dive.abstract: Sensorimotor decisions require balancing extrinsic rewards against intrinsic action costs. How these signals are integrated during rapid eye-movement choices remains unclear. We show that human saccade selection follows cost-benefit computations consistent with neuroeconomic decision-making. Across two experiments, participants maximized rewards and minimized effort-costs. Reward titration revealed that more effortful saccades required higher monetary rewards to be chosen equally often. While effort-costs affected choices linearly, small reward differences shifted choices more than equally-sized larger ones. Furthermore, effort-cost (but not reward) differences regulated decision engagement: Deliberation was extended only when cost differences were large. Together, costs and rewards play dissociable roles in saccade selection, positioning eye movements as a tractable model system for economic decision-making.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: models saccade selection as neuroeconomic decision, linking eye movements to goal-driven choices
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 将扫视选择建模为神经经济决策，链接眼动与目标驱动选择
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 将扫视选择建模为神经经济决策，链接眼动与目标驱动选择
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 将扫视选择建模为神经经济决策，链接眼动与目标驱动选择
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper shows that human saccade selection follows cost-benefit neuroeconomic computations, providing a computational model for understanding goals through eye movements.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究通过两个实验揭示人类扫视选择遵循神经经济学的成本-收益计算：更大努力的眼动需要更高奖励才能被平等选择，且成本差异调节决策参与时间。这为通过眼动理解人类目标提供了计算模型，支持眼动意图识别的理论基础。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该研究通过两个实验揭示人类扫视选择遵循神经经济学的成本-收益计算：更大努力的眼动需要更高奖励才能被平等选择，且成本差异调节决策参与时间。这为通过眼动理解人类目标提供了计算模型，支持眼动意图识别的理论基础。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: computational models for understanding human goals through eye movements

quick_skim.title: ProActor: Timing-Aware Reinforcement Learning for Proactive Task Scheduling Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Proactive task-oriented agents must autonomously anticipate user needs, identify actionable opportunities, and trigger software actions at appropriate moments - fundamentally shifting from reactive systems that await explicit instructions. However, existing approaches lack generalizable end-to-end solutions for measuring and optimizing such anticipatory behaviors.   This paper introduces ProActor, a unified framework for conversational task scheduling that integrates: (1) a domain-agnostic automated annotation methodology that enables scalable proactiveness reinforcement learning (RL) by generating full opportunity time windows instead of rigid point labels, (2) systematic proactiveness metrics capturing both timing quality and reference action alignment, and (3) RL optimization using GRPO with various reward designs. Our insight is that RULER-based rewards with proactiveness rubrics are crucial for improving timing quality, and that proactiveness optimization enabled by stage-aware composite rewards is key to balancing timing quality and reference action alignment.   Timing-aware RL requires extensive exploration, demanding efficient infrastructure. We develop ART-F, an adaptive framework combining request-adaptive inference clusters with DDP-based training on single-node multi-GPU systems, enabling LoRA training of 4-bit Qwen2.5-14B-ProActor-Q4 with 4-8x speedups. Experiments on two newly auto-annotated datasets demonstrate significant improvements in proactive timing while maintaining action consistency comparable to state-of-the-art (SOTA) baselines. Ablations validate the effectiveness of distinct composite reward variations.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Proactive agent using RL to anticipate user needs, transferable to gaze-based interaction
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用强化学习预测用户需求的前瞻性代理，可迁移至基于视线的人机交互
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用强化学习预测用户需求的前瞻性代理，可迁移至基于视线的人机交互
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用强化学习预测用户需求的前瞻性代理，可迁移至基于视线的人机交互
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes ProActor, a timing-aware RL framework for proactive task scheduling agents that anticipate user needs and act autonomously.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有代理系统过于被动，无法在空闲时间预测用户需求。本文提出ProActor框架，通过时序感知强化学习使代理能够自动识别机会窗口并在适当时刻触发行动。该框架包含领域无关的标注方法和系统性主动度量。尽管未涉及眼动数据，但其前瞻性策略可启发基于视线的前瞻交互系统设计。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有代理系统过于被动，无法在空闲时间预测用户需求。本文提出ProActor框架，通过时序感知强化学习使代理能够自动识别机会窗口并在适当时刻触发行动。该框架包含领域无关的标注方法和系统性主动度量。尽管未涉及眼动数据，但其前瞻性策略可启发基于视线的前瞻交互系统设计。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: gaze based interaction paradigms for proactive system response

quick_skim.title: IntentionNav: A Benchmark for Intent-Driven Object Navigation from Implicit Human Instruction
quick_skim.abstract: Existing object navigation benchmarks usually tell an embodied agent which object category to find, such as microwave or chair. Human-facing embodied AI is often asked something less direct: "I need something to warm this food" or "the room feels stuffy." The agent must infer the object that can satisfy the need, find a scene-grounded instance, and decide whether the goal has been reached. We study this setting as intent-driven object navigation and introduce IntentionNav, a diagnostic benchmark for active object search from implicit human instructions. Each episode provides a free-text intent, RGB-D observations, and pose, but withholds the target object name. IntentionNav contains 500 intents over 176 Isaac Sim scenes and 64 target categories. Each intent is rewritten in four controlled instruction styles and annotated with one of four intent modes, separating surface phrasing from semantic cue type under matched geometry. This paired design supports analysis of target inference, language robustness, neighborhood reachability, and terminal success rather than only aggregate success. We evaluated three VLMs using a fixed active-navigation agent. Models identify the intended target in 48.3 percent of episodes and enter its 2 m neighborhood in 68.7 percent, but terminate successfully in only 24.9 percent and achieve grounded 1 m success in 5.5 percent. Success is highest for event-script intents (28.7 percent) and lower for physical-state and affordance intents (19.2 percent and 18.5 percent), showing that indirect human intent remains a bottleneck for target selection, visual verification, and terminal localization in active embodied search.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: intent-driven object navigation from implicit human instruction, involves multimodal intent prediction
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 从隐含人类指令进行意图驱动的物体导航，涉及多模态意图预测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 从隐含人类指令进行意图驱动的物体导航，涉及多模态意图预测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 从隐含人类指令进行意图驱动的物体导航，涉及多模态意图预测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces IntentionNav, a benchmark for active object search from implicit human instructions, where agents must infer goals without explicit object names, using free-text intents and RGB-D observations.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对传统物体导航基准要求明确目标类别的问题，本文提出了IntentionNav基准，让智能体从隐含的人类指令（如“我需要热一下食物”）推断目标物体并进行主动搜索。每个episode提供自由文本意图和RGB-D观测，但不提供目标名称。该基准包含500个意图和176个场景，为评估隐含意图理解能力提供了诊断工具。该工作推动了意图驱动的视觉导航研究。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对传统物体导航基准要求明确目标类别的问题，本文提出了IntentionNav基准，让智能体从隐含的人类指令（如“我需要热一下食物”）推断目标物体并进行主动搜索。每个episode提供自由文本意图和RGB-D观测，但不提供目标名称。该基准包含500个意图和176个场景，为评估隐含意图理解能力提供了诊断工具。该工作推动了意图驱动的视觉导航研究。

quick_skim.title: TRAFA: Anticipating User Actions to Reduce Errors in Procedural Tasks with Predictive Feedback
quick_skim.abstract: Interactive assistance systems typically provide feedback after an action has been completed, supporting error recovery but not preventing the error itself. We present TRAFA, a real-time predictive feedback system for procedural tasks that intervenes before errors are committed. TRAFA operationalizes predictive feedback through a Track-Forecast-Act framework that tracks hand and object state, forecasts user motion conditioned on scene context, and triggers feedback when a predicted action is likely to violate task constraints. We instantiate this pipeline in a sequential assembly setting and evaluate it through both technical benchmarking and a controlled user study against conventional reactive feedback. Our results show that predictive feedback improves task accuracy and efficiency while maintaining a comparable number of feedback events. These findings position feedback timing as a key dimension in system design and show how real-time anticipation can be integrated into interactive systems to prevent errors before they occur.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: real-time predictive feedback system that forecasts user actions to prevent errors, akin to real-time intent recognition
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 实时预测反馈系统，通过预测用户动作预防错误，类似实时意图识别
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 实时预测反馈系统，通过预测用户动作预防错误，类似实时意图识别
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 实时预测反馈系统，通过预测用户动作预防错误，类似实时意图识别
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces TRAFA, a real-time system that tracks hand/object state, forecasts user motion conditioned on scene context, and triggers predictive feedback to prevent errors in procedural tasks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有交互式辅助系统只能在错误发生后提供反馈的问题，本文提出TRAFA实时预测反馈系统。通过追踪手和物体状态并基于场景上下文预测用户运动，在预测到可能违反任务约束时提前触发反馈。在装配任务上的技术评估和用户研究表明，相比传统反应式反馈，预测反馈显著减少了错误次数。该方法可视为前瞻性交互中实时意图识别的实现。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对现有交互式辅助系统只能在错误发生后提供反馈的问题，本文提出TRAFA实时预测反馈系统。通过追踪手和物体状态并基于场景上下文预测用户运动，在预测到可能违反任务约束时提前触发反馈。在装配任务上的技术评估和用户研究表明，相比传统反应式反馈，预测反馈显著减少了错误次数。该方法可视为前瞻性交互中实时意图识别的实现。

quick_skim.title: Anticipate and Learn: Unleashing Idle-Time Compute in Proactive Agents
quick_skim.abstract: While AI agents demonstrate remarkable capabilities in reasoning and tool use, they remain fundamentally reactive: they compute responses only after explicit user prompts. This paradigm ignores a critical opportunity: the idle time between interactions is largely wasted, leaving agents unable to prepare for future user needs. To bridge this gap, we introduce ProAct, a proactive agent architecture that leverages idle-time compute to anticipate and fulfill likely upcoming user needs. By analyzing evolving dialogue history together with persistent memory, ProAct predicts upcoming needs and iteratively acquires information, allowing the agent to resolve knowledge gaps and prepare evidence before the user initiates a query. To rigorously evaluate proactive capabilities, we also introduce ProActEval, a comprehensive benchmark comprising 200 scenarios across 40 domains, featuring predictable need chains and diverse user cognitive profiles. Empirical results demonstrate significant advantages over reactive baselines. ProAct accelerates task completion by reducing required turns by 14.8%, decreases user effort by 11.7%, and cuts hallucination rates by 28.1% on ProActEval. Furthermore, MemBench evaluations confirm that ProAct achieves state-of-the-art reflective accuracy, underscoring its sustained and robust performance.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes proactive agent that anticipates user needs in real-time, relevant to real-time intent recognition algorithms
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出前瞻性智能体实时预测用户需求，与实时意图识别算法相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出前瞻性智能体实时预测用户需求，与实时意图识别算法相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出前瞻性智能体实时预测用户需求，与实时意图识别算法相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ProAct introduces a proactive agent architecture that uses idle-time compute to anticipate user needs, enabling real-time intent forecasting and preparation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: ProAct针对传统智能体被动响应用户查询的局限，提出了一种前瞻性智能体架构。该架构在空闲时分析对话历史和持久记忆，预测用户即将出现的需求，并主动获取相关信息。实验表明，ProAct能够有效缩短用户等待时间并提高响应质量，其预测算法可迁移至眼动意图识别等实时场景。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: ProAct针对传统智能体被动响应用户查询的局限，提出了一种前瞻性智能体架构。该架构在空闲时分析对话历史和持久记忆，预测用户即将出现的需求，并主动获取相关信息。实验表明，ProAct能够有效缩短用户等待时间并提高响应质量，其预测算法可迁移至眼动意图识别等实时场景。
